page: Microsoft Azure E96-24as_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EASv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 24 vCPUs and 12 physical cores operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The server is equipped with an exceptionally large memory capacity of 672.0 GB, translating to 28.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 1376 GB, and the instance is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance scoring in the top 25%. The PassMark memory score also ranks in the top 25%, reflecting high memory performance efficiency. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and static web serving throughput fall within the average range. For machine learning tasks, the instance shows strong performance in text generation using small 135M models, though it exhibits poor performance for prompt processing on large 70B models.

The E96-24as_v4 offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads due to its massive memory-to-vCPU ratio, allowing organizations to run large in-memory databases without paying for unnecessary extra CPU cores. However, the tradeoff lies in its average multi-core scaling and lack of GPU acceleration. This hardware profile is highly suited for memory-intensive enterprise applications, relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and data analytics pipelines.
description: Microsoft Azure E96-24as_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EASv4 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor. It features 24 vCPUs, 12 physical cores running at 3.525 GHz, and a massive 672.0 GB of RAM, providing an exceptional 28.0 GB of memory per core. The server includes 1376 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. Benchmark results highlight strong single-core CPU performance and a top-tier PassMark memory score, while multi-core operations and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. This instance is highly cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads that do not require high CPU core counts. It is best suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring massive RAM capacity and dedicated AMD EPYC processing for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 24 vCPUs and 12 physical cores
Massive 672.0 GB RAM capacity optimized for memory-bound applications
Includes 1376 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core CPU and PassMark memory benchmark performance
Ideal for large in-memory databases, caching, and enterprise applications
